name: Bank Of Billings
description_short: Community bank serving Christian County, Missouri since 1889, offering checking, savings, loans, and investment accounts across two locations.
description_long:
Bank of Billings is the oldest bank in Christian County, Missouri, having operated since 1889. The institution operates two full-service locations in Billings and Marionville, positioning itself as a community-focused alternative to larger regional and national banks. The bank emphasizes personal service through experienced, full-time lenders and trained staff available to help customers find solutions tailored to their needs.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of consumer and business banking products. On the deposit side, they provide checking accounts (with two business options), savings accounts including IRAs and Platinum accounts, and investment products like CDs and money market accounts. For borrowing, they offer auto loans (including boats and trailers), home loans (consumer, real estate, and commercial/agricultural), and specialized commercial/agricultural products including bare land mortgages, construction loans, equipment loans, livestock loans, and lines of credit.

What distinguishes Bank of Billings is its emphasis on community banking and personal relationships. The bank actively promotes services to specific customer segments—students building credit, local business owners seeking flexible loans, and entrepreneurs growing companies. They maintain extended drive-thru hours (8am-5pm Monday-Friday, 8am-12pm Saturday) and operate a community calendar highlighting local events and promotions. The mobile app integration and Card Valet app suggest modern digital banking capabilities alongside traditional service.

The bank's primary limitations stem from its geographic footprint—service is restricted to two Missouri locations, making it inaccessible for customers outside Christian County. For business lending, the documentation requirements are extensive (2 years tax returns, financial statements, insurance proof, corporate documentation), which may deter smaller or newer businesses. Additionally, the website provides limited information about specific interest rates, fees, account minimums, or product terms, requiring direct contact for detailed pricing information. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Business checking accounts (2 types with multiple benefits)
Personal and business savings accounts (including IRAs and Platinum accounts)
Commercial/agricultural loans including bare land mortgages, construction loans, farm land mortgages, equipment loans, and livestock loans
Community calendar and local event promotions
Wire transfers and bill payment services (implied through standard banking operations)
pros: Oldest bank in Christian County (established 1889) with 130+ years of community presence and stability
Two full-service locations with experienced full-time lenders specializing in consumer, commercial, and agricultural lending
Extended drive-thru hours (Saturday service until 12pm) plus extended Friday hours (9am-5pm) for working customers
Specialized loan products for rural/agricultural customers including farm land mortgages, equipment loans, and livestock loans
Multiple checking and savings account options, including business checking with multiple benefits and IRA/Platinum savings accounts
Personalized service model with commitment to creating customized plans for both personal and business customers
Mobile app access for convenience banking alongside in-person service
cons: Limited geographic availability with only two locations in Billings and Marionville, Missouri—not accessible for out-of-state customers
Website lacks specific interest rates, APRs, account minimums, or fee schedules, requiring customers to contact directly for pricing
Restrictive lobby hours (9am-3pm Monday-Thursday) make access difficult for standard 9-to-5 working customers outside of Friday
Commercial/agricultural lending requires extensive documentation (2 years tax returns, financial statements, insurance proof, corporate docs) that may burden small or startup businesses
No indication of online account opening, remote loan applications, or digital-only account options—service appears in-person dependent
best_for: Agricultural and rural business owners seeking specialized farm, equipment, and livestock financing with local relationship banking
Small business owners in Christian County, Missouri who benefit from face-to-face lending and flexible commercial/agricultural products
Long-time community residents building generational relationships with a locally-owned institution
Students and young adults in the area seeking to build credit through basic checking and savings accounts
diagnosis: Bank of Billings is best for established residents and business owners in Christian County, Missouri seeking traditional community banking with agricultural lending expertise and personalized service. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation to two Missouri locations and a website lacking transparent pricing information, requiring direct contact for rate and fee details before commitment.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
